2606.26891.title: Bridging Vision and Language Concepts through Optimal Transport Semantic Flow
2606.26891.paper_summary: Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) promise transparent reasoning by predicting through human-interpretable concepts, yet their effectiveness fundamentally depends on how well visual and textual representations are aligned or matched. Existing vision-language CBMs often rely on pre-aligned encoders or global cosine similarity, which obscures fine-grained concept localization and fails to reflect true semantic geometry. In this work, we rethink concept alignment as a dynamic cross-modal transport process instead of static projection and propose the Optimal Transport Flow Concept Bottleneck Model (OTF-CBM). It first learns a data-driven semantic cost via Inverse Optimal Transport to measure cross-modal distances, and then performs unbalanced optimal-transport-based flow matching to model semantic transitions between visual patches and textual concepts. With velocity-based concept activation, OTF-CBM captures interpretable geometric relations without ODE integration. Experiments further show that OTF-CBM achieves superior classification accuracy and concept faithfulness, offering a new geometric and dynamical perspective for interpretable cross-modal reasoning.
2606.26891.paper_summary_zh: 概念瓶頸模型（CBMs）承諾透過人類可解釋的概念進行透明推理，但它們的有效性根本上取決於視覺和文本表示的對齊或匹配程度。現有的視覺-語言 CBMs 通常依賴於預先對齊的編碼器或全局餘弦相似度，這會模糊細粒度的概念定位，並無法反映真實的語義幾何。在這項工作中，我們將概念對齊重新思考為一個動態的跨模態運輸過程，而不是靜態投影，並提出了最佳運輸流概念瓶頸模型（OTF-CBM）。它首先通過反向最佳運輸學習數據驅動的語義成本，以測量跨模態距離，然後執行不平衡的基於最佳運輸的流匹配，以建模視覺區塊和文本概念之間的語義過渡。通過基於速度的概念激活，OTF-CBM 在不進行常微分方程整合的情況下捕捉可解釋的幾何關係。實驗進一步顯示，OTF-CBM 實現了更優越的分類準確性和概念忠實性，為可解釋的跨模態推理提供了一種新的幾何和動態視角。
2606.26891.author: Chenyang Zhang et.al.
2606.26891.authors: Chenyang Zhang, Anqi Dong, Guangming Zhu, Nuoye Xiong, Siyuan Wang, Lin Mei, Liang Zhang
